operating_state: Total revenues increased to $54.4 billion from $32.0 billion driven by higher crude oil and natural gas realizations across upstream segments. Upstream earnings surged to $6.9 billion from $2.4 billion with both U.S. and international operations contributing materially. Downstream earnings improved to $331 million from $5 million driven by higher U.S. refining margins and increased earnings from Chevron Phillips Chemical Company, though international downstream posted losses due to higher operating expenses.
cost_structure: Purchased crude oil and products costs increased to $32.6 billion from $17.6 billion reflecting higher commodity prices and volumes. Operating expenses rose to $5.6 billion from $5.0 billion primarily due to higher employee benefit expenses, transportation costs, and services fees associated with refinery shutdowns. Depreciation, depletion and amortization decreased to $3.7 billion from $4.3 billion due to lower rates and production volumes.
material_events: Completed acquisition of Renewable Energy Group for $3.15 billion and closed acquisition of Neste's Group III base oil business. Completed sale of Eagle Ford Shale interest in Texas and closed joint venture with Bunge North America for renewable fuel feedstocks. Received 35-year concession for El Trapial field development in Argentina and announced hydrogen fueling sites partnership with Iwatani in California. Declared quarterly dividend of $1.42 per share and repurchased $1.25 billion of common stock.
macro_exposures: Brent crude oil averaged $102 per barrel driven by geopolitical issues including the Ukraine conflict, OPEC+ supply management, and demand recovery from easing COVID-19 restrictions. Supply chain disruptions continue limiting availability of industry inputs with accelerating inflationary pressures on goods and services. CPC pipeline damage in March temporarily curtailed TCO production to 70 percent capacity though repairs restored normal operations by April.
forward_outlook: Capital spending and announced acquisitions expected to exceed 50 percent above 2021 levels including renewable energy acquisitions. Company expects to repurchase $2.5 billion of common stock in Q2 2022 under the $25 billion authorization program with $16.9 billion remaining. Full-year pension plan contributions estimated at $1.3 billion with assets held for sale expected to be divested within 12 months.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple outstanding Notices of Violation at Pasadena refinery will result in civil penalties exceeding $1.0 million under negotiation with Texas Attorney General. Climate change litigation continues across 21 separate lawsuits from U.S. cities, counties, states and other entities seeking damages and equitable relief. The Ecuador arbitration's third phase continues to determine compensation amounts owed by Ecuador to Chevron with tax examinations remaining open across major jurisdictions.
